items.headline: New JACIE IEC Certification Pathway Expands Opportunities for Centers Beyond Transplant
items.preview: The JACIE has launched a new IEC certification pathway, enhancing cellular therapy standards.
items.article:
The field of cellular therapies has reached a significant milestone with the successful completion of the first-ever inspection under the third edition Immune Effector Cells (IEC) Standards by JACIE at Guy’s and St Thomas’ NHS Foundation Trust. This achievement not only confirms the robustness of these newly established standards but also marks a pivotal expansion in the certification of cellular therapy programs beyond the traditional domain of stem cell transplantation.
Why this matters: The newly introduced IEC certification pathway fundamentally broadens JACIE’s portfolio, moving beyond hematopoietic cell transplantation to include therapies for solid tumors and autoimmune diseases. This strategic expansion ensures that quality oversight keeps pace with clinical advances, addressing the complexity and unique challenges inherent in IEC therapy delivery.

items.headline: New Post-Hoc Analysis Explores Daily Oral Orforglipron Use in Adults Over 65 with Obesity, Regardless of Diabetes Status
items.preview: A new analysis highlights the efficacy of orforglipron in older adults for obesity management.
items.article:
A groundbreaking new analysis set to be unveiled at the European Congress on Obesity (ECO 2026) explores the efficacy and safety of orforglipron, an innovative oral GLP-1 receptor agonist, in treating obesity among older adults aged 65 and above. This investigation addresses the ongoing clinical gap regarding incretin-based therapies in geriatric populations, potentially reshaping treatment paradigms for this demographic.
Why this matters: The findings advocate for the integration of oral GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy into standard obesity management algorithms for seniors, highlighting orforglipron’s significant weight and glycemic improvements with a manageable safety profile.

items.headline: Precision in motion: Decoding the critical operations behind the logistics of next-generation cell and gene therapies
items.preview: Logistics for cell and gene therapies face unique challenges requiring specialized solutions.
items.article:
The advent of personalized medicine has brought hope for patients facing rare diseases and previously untreatable cancers. Cell and gene therapies (CGT) present a logistical challenge unlike anything previously seen in the pharmaceutical industry, as they are often patient-specific and highly sensitive to disruptions.
Why this matters: The logistics framework for CGTs must ensure that these delicate lifesaving drugs reach patients at maximum efficacy, necessitating specialized logistics and real-time monitoring to maintain the integrity of the therapies during transport.

items.headline: CRISPR safeguard changes how engineered microbes can be controlled
items.preview: New CRISPR technology offers a more reliable method for controlling engineered microbes.
items.article:
Engineered microorganisms are widely used in industrial biotechnology, but concerns about their uncontrolled proliferation remain. Conventional biocontainment strategies often suffer from limitations, prompting the need for improved methods.
Why this matters: The introduction of an irreversible base-editing safeguard using CRISPR technology provides a more reliable means of controlling microbial survival, enhancing safety in biomanufacturing and potentially improving public trust in engineered microorganisms.

items.headline: The capacity crunch in biopharma is a location problem
items.preview: Biopharma companies are prioritizing location readiness to meet manufacturing demands.
items.article:
Demand for biopharma manufacturing, especially in cell and gene therapy, is outpacing available capacity. Companies are increasingly looking for locations that can support new operations quickly, rather than waiting for new builds to come online.
Why this matters: Location readiness has become a strategic variable for biopharma companies, as it allows for faster operational readiness and access to a skilled workforce, ultimately enabling quicker scaling of emerging therapies.
